expansion_tracker.details: Two issues: (1) MAJOR: next_id=365 but live_max=369 — allocator undercount, next allocation will collide with existing EXP-365 through EXP-369. Root cause: a batch writer (EXP-369 pickup cluster of 8 ISS sub-entries) was added after next_id was set, and next_id was not advanced. (2) MODERATE: 42 live items with terminal status (complete+integrated or cancelled) not moved to archive — predicate violation. Count grew from 9 (2026-05-13 report) to 42.

issues_found.description: expansion-tracker.json next_id=365 is LESS THAN live max EXP-369. A batch writer created EXP-365 through EXP-369 (and 8 EXP-369-pickup-ISS-XXXX sub-entries) without advancing next_id past 369. The next analyst allocation will collide with an existing entry.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Decider should patch next_id to at least 370 (i.e., max(live_max, arch_max)+1 = 370). Also investigate EXP-369-pickup-* sub-entries — these are non-standard IDs that bypass the allocator.

issues_found.description: 42 live expansion-tracker items have terminal status (complete+integrated or cancelled/superseded) and have not been moved to archive. Count grew from 9 yesterday to 42 today. This violates the live-state predicate and creates noise for agents reading the live array.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Verifier or decider should move all items with integrated=true or status in [cancelled,superseded,subsumed] from live items[] to expansion-tracker-archive.jsonl. Affected EXPs: EXP-308 through EXP-369 (many). The PROP-022 phase 5 verifier handles this — may need a triggered run.

issues_found.description: EXP-052, EXP-300, EXP-359 absent from both live items and archive. Unchanged from previous report — likely cancelled allocations. next_id=365 is now BELOW max (not above), so this gap report is secondary to the major allocator issue.
issues_found.suggested_fix: Informational only given major allocator issue takes precedence. No action needed on gaps themselves.

summary: Overall status WARN. Yesterday's CRITICAL build drift is fully resolved — HTML rebuilt at 01:08Z (decider acted on integrity alert), wrong DOI and author corrected, test.js passes all 3606 checks. One new MAJOR: expansion-tracker next_id=365 is below live max=369, meaning the next EXP allocation will collide with an existing entry. Root cause appears to be a batch writer (analyst created EXP-369 plus 8 EXP-369-pickup-ISS-XXXX sub-entries in a single run) that advanced EXP IDs without updating next_id. Decider should patch next_id to 370 immediately. One MODERATE: 42 live tracker items with terminal status (up from 9 yesterday) violating the live-state predicate — archive move needed. Curmudgeon digest clean (0 parse errors, 4 unprocessed in normal lag). All external data source links reachable. Discoverability infrastructure intact. Hardcoded colors clean. Drift audit fresh at 43.9h.
